state_refinement.release_state: This PR adds MCP-local, host-specific adapter install state only: not_installed -> enabled (install with consent) <-> disabled (disable/enable) and uninstall -> not_installed. Pre-work invocation is exactly-once per task-boundary correlation id, enforced by local dedup markers. No canonical daemon lifecycle, Decision, candidate, signoff, evidence, or compliance state is added or changed; MCP remains a transport surface that forwards bounded context and renders daemon verdicts.
states_deferred: daemon-side dedup/idempotent consumption of correlation_id/idempotency_key (out of scope; enforced locally by MCP dedup markers for now)
invariants: Canonical Decision/candidate/signoff/evidence/compliance state, authority, and persistence semantics are unchanged; the daemon remains the sole owner.
Automatic preflight fires only at a genuine pre-work boundary (source == startup) and exactly once per correlation id (host:session_id); it never runs mid-session or pre-write.
Only bounded, allowlisted context is forwarded; raw transcripts, secrets, unrelated tool output, environment, and telemetry are never read or sent.
A missing host mechanism, unavailable daemon, protocol mismatch, or dispatch error yields a visible typed fallback that never claims preflight ran and never consumes the once-per-boundary marker.
Enabling or a positive capability probe for one host never implies support or evidence for another host.
Adapters never render, choose, promote, or confirm candidates and make no compliance, safety, no-conflict, or merge-readiness claims.
The Bicameral Factory is never installed, imported, fetched, or read at product runtime.

rejected_or_collapsed_states: explicit 'installed_active' vs 'installed_enabled' split (collapsed into a single enabled state)
separate 'consent_pending' state (collapsed into a consent gate on install; consent is a recorded attribute, not a lifecycle state)
per-invocation 'fired'/'pending'/'failed' state machine (collapsed into local dedup markers plus a typed fallback outcome; fallbacks intentionally do not persist a fired marker)
minimization_review.rationale: The smallest honest state machine is the three local install states plus a correlation-keyed exactly-once guard. Invocation results are transient outcomes, not durable states; consent is an attribute of an enabled adapter. This avoids inventing canonical lifecycle state that the daemon, not MCP, must own.
